title: Setting Up a Rust CLI Project
content:
Rust is an excellent choice for CLI tools: fast startup, small binaries, no runtime dependencies, and great argument parsing libraries.

Start with `cargo new my-tool` and add clap for argument parsing plus anyhow for error handling. These two crates form the foundation of nearly every Rust CLI.

Organize your project with main.rs as a thin entry point that parses args and calls into lib.rs where the logic lives. This separation makes testing easier and allows your CLI logic to be used as a library.
keyPoints: Use `cargo new` to create a new CLI project
clap + anyhow are the standard foundation for Rust CLIs
Separate main.rs (arg parsing) from lib.rs (logic) for testability
Return Result from main() for clean error output
